items.prompt: In Example 9.28, which step follows collecting tweets via a streaming API?
choices: Handling continuous flows (e.g., Kafka/Flume), then NLP sentiment and dashboards
Storing only monthly CSV exports with no processing
items.explain: The case study chains collection, streaming transport, sentiment NLP, and visualization.

items.prompt: HDFS and similar distributed stores split data across nodes to support parallel processing and scalable storage.
items.explain: Section 9.4.6 describes distributed file systems for fault-tolerant, scalable big-data storage.

items.prompt: Which pair correctly contrasts ingestion modes in this part?
choices: Batch: periodic large chunks; real-time: continuous streaming as data are generated
Batch: only edge encryption; real-time: only paper forms
Real-time ingestion cannot use Kafka-like tools
items.explain: Batch is periodic; real-time streams continuously with tools such as Kafka, Flume, or Kinesis.

items.prompt: Name one data-quality issue common in big-data collection according to this part.
items.rubric: Accept missing data, inconsistent formats/units/terminology, or duplicate entries.
items.sample_answer: Missing sensor readings after network failures, or inconsistent formats across sources, or duplicate records.

items.prompt: Emerging trends discussed include edge computing and AI-driven analytics for big-data workloads.
items.explain: The outlook covers edge computing, 5G, quantum directions, and AI-driven analytics among future trends.
